comment_text.sn35.71:2.2: Commentary traces anassasaṁ (variant anassāsiṁ) to √nas (perish), which Digital Pali Dictionary rejects as “unviable”. It is, rather, the present participle of na assasati, “discouraged, unconsoled”.
comment_text.sn35.71:5.1: The Buddha encourages with the future tense, rather than the optative “should”. This is an example of his knowledge of where all paths leads.

root_text.sn35.71:1.1: “Yo hi koci, bhikkhave, bhikkhu channaṁ phassāyatanānaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.
root_text.sn35.71:1.2: Avusitaṁ tena brahmacariyaṁ, ārakā so imasmā dhammavinayā”ti.
root_text.sn35.71:2.1: Evaṁ vutte, aññataro bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.71:2.3: Ahañhi, bhante, channaṁ phassāyatanānaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāmī”ti.
root_text.sn35.71:3.2: cakkhuṁ ‘etaṁ mama, esohamasmi, eso me attā’ti samanupassasī”ti?
root_text.sn35.71:5.1: “Sādhu, bhikkhu, ettha ca te, bhikkhu, cakkhu ‘netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attā’ti evametaṁ yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya sudiṭṭhaṁ bhavissati.
root_text.sn35.71:5.3: jivhaṁ ‘etaṁ mama, esohamasmi, eso me attā’ti samanupassasī”ti?
root_text.sn35.71:7.1: “Sādhu, bhikkhu, ettha ca te, bhikkhu, jivhā ‘netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attā’ti evametaṁ yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya sudiṭṭhaṁ bhavissati.
root_text.sn35.71:7.3: manaṁ ‘etaṁ mama, esohamasmi, eso me attā’ti samanupassasī”ti?
root_text.sn35.71:9.1: “Sādhu, bhikkhu, ettha ca te, bhikkhu, mano ‘netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attā’ti evametaṁ yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya sudiṭṭhaṁ bhavissati.

translation_text.sn35.71:1.1: “Mendicants, anyone who doesn’t truly understand the six fields of contact’s origin, disappearance, gratification, drawback, and escape
translation_text.sn35.71:1.2: has not completed the spiritual journey and is far from this teaching and training.”
translation_text.sn35.71:2.1: When he said this, one of the mendicants said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn35.71:2.3: For I don’t truly understand the six fields of contact’s origin, disappearance, gratification, drawback, and escape.”
translation_text.sn35.71:3.2: Do you regard the eye like this: ‘This is mine, I am this, this is my self’?”
translation_text.sn35.71:5.1: “Good, mendicant! And regarding the eye, you will truly see clearly with right wisdom that: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’
translation_text.sn35.71:5.2: Just this is the end of suffering.
translation_text.sn35.71:5.3: Do you regard the ear … nose … tongue … body …
translation_text.sn35.71:7.3: Do you regard the mind like this: ‘This is mine, I am this, this is my self’?”
translation_text.sn35.71:9.1: “Good, mendicant! And regarding the mind, you will truly see clearly with right wisdom that: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’
translation_text.sn35.71:9.2: Just this is the end of suffering.”
